genre_refs: rogues-gallery / 'Worst of the Week' recurring series
description: A designed vertical 'rogues-gallery' card from ICE's recurring 'Worst of the Week' series. On a black ribbed/striped background, twelve booking-style mugshot photographs of detained individuals are arranged around the edges in roughly four rows, each framed with a dark caption bar stating an alleged crime. Labels include (top row) 'Deadly Conduct', 'Aggravated Assault', 'Assault', 'Assault'; (next) 'Robbery', 'Deadly Conduct'; (next) 'Cocaine Possession', 'Sex w/ Minor'; (bottom row) 'Hit-and-Run', 'Rape', 'Fraud', 'Child Sexual Assault'. The subjects are mostly men of varying apparent ethnicities. Centered is the large white serif title 'Worst' over 'Week' with 'of the' in white on a red angled banner. Bottom-left is a red banner with the URL 'ice.gov/WoW'; bottom-right is the DHS seal and white 'ICE' wordmark. Style is a tabloid 'most-dangerous offenders' collage. Messaging technique: criminalization and fear amplification, displaying immigrant detainees as a wall of violent and sexual offenders to justify aggressive enforcement and removals. Target audience: general public, to associate immigration with serious crime.
notable_text: Worst of the Week; Deadly Conduct; Aggravated Assault; Assault; Robbery; Cocaine Possession; Sex w/ Minor; Hit-and-Run; Rape; Fraud; Child Sexual Assault; ice.gov/WoW
